user_message: I have this exec meeting notes from Lucerne Publishing that needs build the narrative. Renewal conversation is tomorrow.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-10 17:17 EST
Subject: Follow-up: executive brief Review

Just checking in on the executive brief proposal. Our Head of Digital is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
Date: 2024-01-01 08:48 EST
Subject: Follow-up: executive brief Review

Our team encountered some challenges with executive brief. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Lucerne Publishing: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF37-C trap (Under-routes a serious outage); produces complete Severity triage, root-cause hypothesis (labeled as hypothesis), customer response draft, ACR risk fl
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF37-C failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF37-C failure mode (Under-routes a serious outage)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct executive brief decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF37-C -- Under-routes a serious outage
